iran_nuke.notes: Nuclear track still de-escalated following Oman agreement. However, Israel confirmed killing top Iranian nuclear scientists in recent strikes — potential long-term catalyst. New Supreme Leader Khamenei has not made nuclear rhetoric. No enrichment escalation signals. Netanyahu veiled threat to Khamenei is political, not nuclear-specific. No new nuclear signals.

iran_conventional.notes: Day 14 — MIXED SIGNALS. Iran laying naval mines in Strait of Hormuz confirmed. Two fuel tankers struck in Iraqi waters by explosive-laden Iranian boats. US-owned tanker Safesea Vishnu struck. Three additional cargo ships hit by unknown projectiles in/near Hormuz overnight. Iraq's oil ports completely stopped operations. Iran warns world to prepare for $200 oil. NEW: Iran threatens to 'set region's oil and gas on fire' if energy infrastructure attacked — major escalation rhetoric. Treasury Sec Bessent announced US Navy WILL escort tankers through Hormuz 'as soon as militarily possible' — first concrete commitment to escorts, partially reversing prior refusal. Oil up 9%. US military refueling plane crashes in Iraq (5 onboard). US struck ~6,000 targets in Iran, cost $11.3B in 6 days. New Supreme Leader vows Hormuz stays closed. Situation volatile — shipping attacks widening but US now committing to escort operations.

israel_lebanon.notes: Day 13 — ESCALATING. Hezbollah launched ~200 rockets into northern Israel. IDF struck 10 Hezbollah facilities in Dahieh (Beirut suburbs) in 30 minutes including intelligence HQ and command centers. Lebanon death toll at 687, 800,000+ displaced. IDF chief says war on Hezbollah 'will not be short, more troops to north.' Netanyahu says Israel is 'crushing' Iranian regime AND Hezbollah. Israel striking both Beirut AND Tehran simultaneously. 25-mile evacuation order for southern Lebanon standing. Washington Post: conflict pushing Lebanon to 'new precipice.'

turkey.notes: Iranian missiles intercepted over Turkish airspace (2 incidents). NATO Article 4 discussions. Erdogan warns Iran but insists Turkey is 'on side of peace.' NATO forces building up off coast of Cyprus. Iranian drone hit UK base in Cyprus. No Article 5 or mobilization. No significant changes.

india.notes: No India-Pakistan border buildup. Pakistan deployed army to Gilgit-Skardu to suppress pro-Iran protests. Shiite demonstrators attacked UN observer offices in Azad Kashmir. No bilateral escalation or nuclear rhetoric. Stable.

russia.notes: Russia calls on US/Israel to stop attacks and negotiate. Kremlin signals intent to expand demands on Ukraine/NATO. Europe alarmed US spending ME-bound weapons that were earmarked for Ukraine. Russia continues arms supply to Iran. No direct ME military involvement. ISW reports Putin strengthening internet censorship — possible preparation for future NATO conflict. No new escalation signals.

china.notes: Chinese flights around Taiwan resumed March 13 after ~2-week pause. Trump delaying Taiwan arms sale for Trump-Xi summit (Mar 31-Apr 2). De-escalatory posture. DPRK condemns US ME attacks — China notably silent. No new escalation signals.

north_korea.notes: DPRK Foreign Ministry condemned US-Israel attacks on Iran as 'destroying regional peace.' Kim inspected weapons factory. No missile or nuclear tests. DPRK aligning rhetoric with China/Russia anti-US position but no military action. Stable.

russia_ukraine.notes: Occupies ~20% Ukraine. 128 combat clashes on March 11. Russia launched 103 airstrikes, dropped 322 guided aerial bombs, deployed 9,216 kamikaze drones in 24h. Scorched-earth attacks on energy infrastructure pushing Ukraine power grid to near collapse. Putin signed decree making passportization of occupied territories permanent. Russia pounding Ukrainian energy and transport infrastructure. US weapons diverted to ME alarming European allies. Coalition of 34 countries now providing all military support to Ukraine (US largely diverted). Russia exploiting US distraction.

top_signals: Iran laying naval mines in Strait of Hormuz — confirmed by US officials
Two fuel tankers struck in Iraqi waters by explosive-laden Iranian boats
Three additional cargo ships hit by unknown projectiles in/near Hormuz overnight
US-owned tanker Safesea Vishnu struck off Iraqi coast in Persian Gulf
Iraq's oil ports completely stopped operations after attack on fuel tanker
Iran threatens to 'set region's oil and gas on fire' if energy infrastructure attacked
Iran warns world to prepare for $200 oil — oil up 9%, highest in ~4 years
US Navy to escort tankers through Hormuz 'as soon as militarily possible' — Bessent
US military refueling plane crashes in Iraq (5 onboard)
Hezbollah launched ~200 rockets into northern Israel
IDF chief: war on Hezbollah 'will not be short, more troops to north'
Russia launched 103 airstrikes, 322 guided bombs, 9,216 drones in 24h on Ukraine

new: Three cargo ships hit by unknown projectiles in/near Strait of Hormuz — shipping attacks widening
Iran threatens to 'set region's oil and gas on fire' if energy infrastructure attacked — major escalation rhetoric
US Navy to escort tankers through Hormuz 'as soon as militarily possible' — Bessent reverses prior refusal
removed: US Navy refusing escort requests — now committed to escort 'when militarily possible'

latest_news.headline: US Navy to escort tankers through Strait of Hormuz — Bessent
latest_news.text: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told Sky News that US Navy will escort vessels through Strait of Hormuz 'as soon as it is militarily possible.' First concrete commitment to escort operations, partially reversing prior refusal.

latest_news.headline: Iran threatens to 'set region's oil and gas on fire' if energy infrastructure attacked
latest_news.text: Iran issued new threat to set the region's oil and gas infrastructure on fire if its own energy facilities are struck. Major escalation rhetoric targeting broader Gulf energy infrastructure beyond Hormuz.

latest_news.headline: Three cargo ships hit by unknown projectiles in/near Strait of Hormuz overnight
latest_news.text: Three cargo ships reported being hit by unknown projectiles in and near the Strait of Hormuz. Shipping attacks continuing to widen beyond earlier incidents.

latest_news.headline: Oil up 9% — Iran warns world to prepare for $200/barrel
latest_news.text: Global oil prices settled up 9%, highest in nearly 4 years. Iran warned world to prepare for $200/barrel. Analysts: unlikely to hit $200 but sustained $130-150 range possible if conflict drags.

latest_news.headline: US military refueling plane crashes in Iraq — 5 onboard
latest_news.text: US refueling plane crashed in Iraq with 5 personnel onboard. Rescue effort underway. Cause not yet determined — combat damage or mechanical failure. First significant US operational loss.

latest_news.headline: First 6 days of Iran war cost US $11.3 billion, Pentagon says
latest_news.text: Pentagon told Congress first 6 days cost $11.3B, excluding buildup costs. US has struck about 6,000 targets in Iran. Enormous expenditure with no sign of quick resolution.

latest_news.headline: Hezbollah launches ~200 rockets into northern Israel — IDF strikes Dahieh
latest_news.text: Hezbollah fired approximately 200 rockets into northern Israel. IDF struck 10 Hezbollah facilities in Dahieh (Beirut suburbs) in 30 minutes including intelligence HQ and command centers. Lebanon death toll: 687.

latest_news.headline: IDF chief: war on Hezbollah 'will not be short, more troops to north'
latest_news.text: IDF Chief of Staff says Lebanon campaign will not be short and more troops being deployed north. Signals major ground operation expansion. Netanyahu says Israel is 'crushing' both Iran and Hezbollah simultaneously.

outlook: CRITICAL with MIXED SIGNALS: Iran has escalated from blockading Hormuz to actively mining it AND attacking tankers in Iraqi waters with explosive boats — attacks widening across the Persian Gulf. Three more cargo ships hit overnight. Iran now threatens to 'set region's oil and gas on fire' — targeting ALL Gulf energy infrastructure. However, Treasury Sec Bessent committed to US Navy escort operations 'as soon as militarily possible' — first concrete step to restore shipping. US-owned tanker struck. Iraq's oil ports shut down. Oil up 9%. US military aircraft loss (refueling plane in Iraq). Cost $11.3B for 6 days. Lebanon front: 200-rocket Hezbollah barrage, IDF signals long campaign. Both fronts escalating simultaneously. Russia exploiting with massive Ukraine assault (322 guided bombs, 9,216 drones). Watch for: actual start of US escort operations, Iran response to escorts (mines vs warships clash), oil crossing $150, Iran nuclear posture after scientist killings, Iran retaliation against Gulf energy infrastructure beyond Hormuz.
